QF.id: Terminology in Equity and Debt Markets_34

question.text: A puttable bond gives the right to:

answer.text: The issuer to call back the bond early
The bondholder to demand early repayment from the issuer
The regulator to cancel the bond
A third party to convert the bond to equity

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A puttable bond gives the bondholder the right to demand early repayment from the issuer, usually at par, before the stated maturity. **The Trap:** A callable bond gives the issuer the right. Puttable is the opposite — it protects the bondholder who can exit if conditions deteriorate. **Cross-Exam Context:** Bond features are tested in NISM Series XV and IV. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Puttable bonds are attractive when investors fear rising rates — they can sell back the bond and reinvest at higher rates.
